title: ceratostylis hydrophila
intro: Ceratostylis hydrophila, also known as the Slipper Orchid, is a species of flowering plant in the orchid family. The Slipper Orchid has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Malaysia and the Philippines, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiparasitic effects. The plant's extracts and compounds have been found to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating pain, and treating skin conditions, among others. In herbal preparations, the Slipper Orchid is often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
